reasoning: The Australian federal election has been called for May 3, 2025. Following a period in late 2024 and early 2025 where polling was practically tied or showed a slight Coalition lead, recent polling in April (including Newspoll and Essential) has swung back to Labor, with the latest numbers indicating a 52-48 lead for the incumbent government on a two-party preferred basis. Historically, first-term federal governments in Australia have an extremely high re-election rate (the last to lose was in 1931). The Coalition currently starts from a low floor of 58 seats, requiring a formidable net gain of 18 seats to form a majority, and it is largely unable to retrieve the formerly safe seats lost to 'Teal' independents in 2022. Even in the event of a hung parliament, Labor has a drastically easier path to forming a minority government because the crossbench of Teals and Greens is ideologically more aligned with Labor on key issues like climate change, and Peter Dutton has ruled out negotiating with many of them. Betting markets (such as Sportsbet) and prediction platforms (like Polymarket) align closely with these fundamentals, implying roughly a 25-28% chance for a Coalition victory. Given the favorable polling trend for Labor three weeks out, the prohibitive electoral math for the Coalition, and the historical base rate, a probability of 0.23 is appropriate.
